title: quercus × sublaurifolia
intro: Quercus × sublaurifolia, also known as the hybrid white oak, is a cross between Quercus coccinea and Quercus laurifolia, resulting in a unique and adaptable tree species. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues due to its high content of tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus × sublaurifolia include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for cardiovascular health and anti-aging. Quercus × sublaurifolia is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and its leaves and bark are also used to make tinctures and infusions, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to promote healing and relaxation.
